violations.text: No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by buckets and trashcan. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. PIC cleared a path to handsink. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

Soup was observed held at 128 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated soup. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

Inspector observed kimchi fermenting in kitchen area. Per interview with staff, establishment makes their own kimchi. Establishment to submit documentation/procedure of the kimchi making process, including full list of ingredients, measurements, and detailed step by step process to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026
The handwashing sink in the kitchen is lacking proper signage. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Working containers of (dry ingredients) are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Buffet line items do not contain a sign with a list of ingredients and allergens. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
(Food ) were observed stored on the floor in the (walk in freezer ). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Single service items were observed on the floor in the basement area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The following non food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust,dirt, food residue, and/or other debris: 1.refrigerator units 2.prep units 3.hood vents 4.walk in cooler air vents 5.table under range top. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Menu was lacking an allergen statement. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Food scraps and sponge were observed in the hand-washing sinks. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed and removed items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking proper hand-washing signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Lemons were observed in direct contact with raw eggs in the prep unit. PIC disposed of lemons. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as produce) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated eggs. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service. Service arrived and fixed the dishwasher during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer and ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took items out of service to be washed/sanitized. PIC disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

A paint can was stored with and above food. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. PIC relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Employees' medicines were not stored to prevent the contamination of food, equipment, or utensils. PIC relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
The buffet line items do not contain a sign with a list of ingredients and allergens. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer and kitchen area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Inspector observed knifes stored between prep unit and table. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

Utensils (ladles) in the self service area are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. PIC flipped ladles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Single service items were observed on the floor in the basement area. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

- Wood is used in the construction of cleaning tools at the 3 bay sink and dishwashing machine. - Cardboard is used as shelf liners above range top.Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and/or other debris: - refrigerator units - prep units - hood vents - walk-in air vents - table under range top New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
3 bay sink was lacking drain plugs. PIC obtained drain plugs during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
Grease trap was observed in poor repair in basement area. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
The ceilings in kitchen area is in poor repair (previous water damage). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024

Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (produce) in the walk-in and prep unit refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit taken out of service to be washed and sanitized. Ice disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

Various food product was observed held at (47-51) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

The door to the outside, located in the (kitchen), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the food storage shelves in the kitchen, stove hoods, and fans and fan base in walk in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024
Outside dumpster area observed with trash, litter, and other debris. Refuse areas must be kept clean as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the (basement) area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024

The floors in walk coolers have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024
The floor and wall junctures in the (kitchen) do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the (kitchen) area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 08/09/2024
